of their time to aid worthy cause.
Others had their origin in various
sources. some being clipped from
financial papers. Neither the bust
ness men nor the financial papers
got any of that 5000 per month, and
the question Is who got $500 for DO
ing what any newspaper man in the
city would be glad to do for $40 per
week?


Anyway. the result has not been
satisfactory. MS the following self
explanatory circular letter shows:


SPOKANE, Wash, June 20. 1908.
Dear Sir: The following subscrib-
ers to the agreement Of January 20.
1903, in reference to special articles
In regard to Spokane. have served
notice upon the Spokesman Review in
accordance with the terms Of agree
ment referred to. that they will. after
80 days from June 20. 1903. decline
to continue the matter further. d. T.
Ham. 9. Heath. The Big Bend Land
company. Fred d. Chamberlin. Louis
ziegler. G. d. Bibbins, The Washing
ton Water Power company"
